questions.prompt: What is the notice primarily about?

passage.text:
Attention Residents!
Due to necessary maintenance on the main water line, there will be a temporary interruption in water service for all
residents in the Maplewood dormitory.
Date: Monday, March 15 Time: 9:00 A.M.-4:00 P.M.
Important Information:
1. Preparation:
• Fill containers with water for drinking and cooking before the interruption.
• Store enough water for personal hygiene needs.
2. During the Interruption:
• Do not attempt to use faucets, showers, or any appliances that require water.
• Toilets may not function properly; please plan accordingly.
3. After Service Resumes:
• Run cold water through all faucets for a few minutes to clear any sediment.
• Check for any leaks and report them immediately to the maintenance department.
Contact Information: For questions or concerns, please contact the Maplewood Maintenance Department at 555-
4321.
We apologize for the inconvenience and appreciate your understanding as we work to improve our community's
water infrastructure.
2026#HM13
Home

Join the Annual Cultural Festival!
Date: August 20 | Time: 10:00 A.M. - 6:00 P.M.
Location: Central Park in Glenn Valley
Celebrate the diversity and richness of our community at the Annual
Cultural Festival! Enjoy a day filled with exciting activities and
performances suitable for all ages. Here are some of the highlights:
• Live Music and Dance Performances:
Experience traditional and contemporary music and dance
from various cultures.
• Food Stalls:
Taste delicious dishes from around the world, including
Spanish tapas, Italian pasta, Indian curry, and more.
• Art Exhibits:
View artworks from local and international artists showcasing
different styles and mediums.
• Children's Activities:
Bring the kids for face painting, storytelling sessions,
and interactive games.
• Cultural Workshops:
Participate in workshops on crafts, cooking, and languages led by
cultural experts.
Admission is free for everyone.
Don't miss out on this incredible event!

questions.prompt: The word "norms" in the passage is closest in meaning to

passage.text:
Social role theory is a significant framework in theoretical

sociology, explaining how individuals adopt roles based on societal expectations. Society is structured by roles, each with specific behaviors and norms. A key aspect of social role

theory is role conflict, which occurs when the expectations of

one role clash with those of another. For instance, a person

who is both a parent and an employee might struggle to balance caring for their children and meeting work obligations. This conflict can lead to stress and decreased performance in

one or both roles. Another important concept is role strain, which is stress within a single role due to competing demands

or insufficient resources. An example is a teacher who feels

overwhelmed by the need to manage classroom behavior while also keeping lessons engaging. Social role theory also

addresses how roles change over time. As society evolves,

the expectations attached to roles can shift. For example, the role of women in the workforce has changed dramatically, with increasing acceptance of women in leadership. Understanding

these dynamics is crucial for analyzing how social structures

impact individual behavior and societal change.

2026#H1113

Home Module1 - Router

questions.prompt: Why does the passage mention "a person who is both a parent and an employee"?

passage.text: Social role theory is a significant framework in theoretical sociology, explaining how individuals adopt roles based on societal expectations. Society is structured by roles, each with specific behaviors and norms. A key aspect of social role theory is role conflict, which occurs when the expectations of one role clash with those of another. For instance, a person who is both a parent and an employee might struggle to balance caring for their children and meeting work obligations. This conflict can lead to stress and decreased performance in one or both roles. Another important concept is role strain, which is stress within a single role due to competing demands or insufficient resources. An example is a teacher who feels overwhelmed by the need to manage classroom behavior while also keeping lessons engaging. Social role theory also addresses how roles change over time. As society evolves, the expectations attached to roles can shift. For example, the role of women in the workforce has changed dramatically, with increasing acceptance of women in leadership. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for analyzing how social structures impact individual behavior and societal change.

options.text: To show how a person can balance multiple obligations
To imply that roles are not always rigidly defined
To illustrate the concept of role conflict
To emphasize the importance of good childcare

passage.text: Social role theory is a significant framework in theoretical sociology, explaining how individuals adopt roles based on societal expectations. Society is structured by roles, each with specific behaviors and norms. A key aspect of social role theory is role conflict, which occurs when the expectations of one role clash with those of another. For instance, a person who is both a parent and an employee might struggle to balance caring for their children and meeting work obligations. This conflict can lead to stress and decreased performance in one or both roles. Another important concept is role strain, which is stress within a single role due to competing demands or insufficient resources. An example is a teacher who feels overwhelmed by the need to manage classroom behavior while also keeping lessons engaging. Social role theory also addresses how roles change over time. As society evolves, the expectations attached to roles can shift. For example, the role of women in the workforce has changed dramatically, with increasing acceptance of women in leadership. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for analyzing how social structures impact individual behavior and societal change.

questions.directive: Read the passage and choose the best answer.
questions.prompt: Hide According to the passage, balancing the ensemble is a challenge for wind ensemble conductors because

passage.text: In the world of classical music, wind ensemble conducting requires a blend of artistry and precision, demanding a conductor who can shape diverse musicians into a cohesive unit. Unlike orchestral conductors, who often lead ensembles centered around strings, wind ensemble conductors primarily work with woodwinds, brass, and percussion, requiring a different set of interpretive and technical skills. One challenge of wind ensemble conducting is balancing the ensemble. Wind instruments produce a wide range of dynamics and timbres, making it essential for conductors to ensure that no single section overpowers the others. For example, the brassy sound of trumpets can dominate if not carefully managed. Conductors must use gestures and cues to achieve balance, considering the acoustics of the performance space. Interpreting the score is another crucial aspect. Wind ensemble pieces often include compositions from modern and contemporary composers, which can be complex and unconventional. Conductors need to interpret these scores carefully and convey their vision to the musicians. This involves precise beat patterns and expressive gestures to communicate dynamic contrasts and phrasing. Wind ensemble conductors collaborate closely with composers, which can lead to unique performances that reflect the composer's original intent. Conductors may need to adjust their style to suit different compositions and settings.

options.text: woodwind sections typically need more rehearsal time than brass sections do
wind instruments create a wide variety of sound qualities and volumes
the cues and gestures used by wind ensemble conductors are limited
most performance spaces have poor acoustics
